title: Reaction Kinetics and Rate Laws
description: Pedagogical pathway from measurable rate to mechanism-based rate laws

edges.label: if a reactant concentration drops from 0.80 M to 0.50 M in 10 s, the average reaction rate is ___
edges.hint: Average rate uses the positive quantity -Δ[A]/Δt = -(0.50 - 0.80)/10.

edges.label: in the expression rate = -Δ[A]/Δt, the quantity in brackets has units of ___
edges.hint: Square brackets denote molar concentration; the rate itself has units of mol L⁻¹ s⁻¹.

edges.label: to determine the exponent on [A] in the rate law, you vary [A] while holding other concentrations ___ across trials
edges.hint: Changing one variable at a time lets you isolate its effect on the rate.

edges.label: for overall reactions, the rate law is determined ___
edges.hint: It is usually not read directly from overall stoichiometric coefficients.

edges.label: if a rate law is \(rate = k[A]^1[B]^2\), the overall order is ___
edges.hint: Overall order is the sum of the exponents on concentration terms: 1 + 2 = 3.

edges.label: for a first-order rate law \(rate = k[A]\), the units of k are ___
edges.hint: Rate has units of concentration per time, so for first order k must have units of inverse time.

edges.label: in the Arrhenius relationship, the rate constant k depends strongly on ___
edges.hint: Arrhenius links k to absolute temperature through an exponential factor.

edges.label: according to the Arrhenius equation, doubling the absolute temperature causes k to ___
edges.hint: Higher T makes the exponent less negative, so the exponential factor grows and k increases.

edges.label: on a plot of \ln(k)\ versus \1/T\, a larger activation barrier gives a ___ slope
edges.hint: Since slope = -E_a/R, increasing E_a makes the slope decrease further below zero.

edges.label: many overall reactions proceed through elementary ___
edges.hint: The summed elementary steps reproduce the net reaction.

edges.label: among all elementary steps in a mechanism, the overall rate is controlled by the ___ step
edges.hint: Think of it as a bottleneck: the slowest step limits how fast products accumulate.

edges.label: for multistep mechanisms, the final rate law may also include terms from earlier ___
edges.hint: A fast pre-equilibrium can introduce concentration terms not visible in the RDS alone.

edges.label: in a catalytic mechanism, the catalyst is consumed and then ___
edges.hint: It participates in steps but is not used up overall.

edges.label: a catalyst speeds reaction by making the Arrhenius exponent less ___ at the same temperature
edges.hint: Lowering E_a makes -E_a/(RT) less negative, so k increases.
